channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: America’s Biggest Wind Farm Arrives Just as Industry Heads for Declines
item.description: The pace of onshore turbine installations is set to slow until 2030 as the renewables sector faces a myriad of headwinds.
item.dc:creator: Mark Chediak

item.title: How Paris Is Using the Seine to Try to Keep Cool in Scorching Heat
item.description: Chilled water and miles of pipes create eco-friendly air conditioning for the city
item.dc:creator: Francois de Beaupuy

item.title: Shark Attacks Prompt Daily Drone Monitoring of Sydney Beaches
item.description: Sydney’s beaches will have daily dawn-to-dusk drone monitoring for sharks starting in July, following a spate of attacks off the Australian coast.
item.dc:creator: Ainslie Chandler

item.title: How AI-Powered Public Comments Are Entering US Climate Politics
item.description: Officials have been confounded by the new phenomenon.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Chen, Todd Woody, Zahra Hirji

item.title: Europe’s Heat Wave Raises the Cost of Waiting to Adapt
item.description: The early, intense heat wave sweeping across Europe is testing which systems can still cope — and forcing difficult questions about whether to endure more extreme temperatures or invest billions in adapting to a hotter future.
item.dc:creator: Joe Wertz
